title: Security Role Changes vs RACI in ServiceNow CMDB Ownership

compliance.assurance_rationale: ISO/IEC 27001 A.5.2 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that ISO 27001 A.5.2 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.6.10: Security Role Changes vs RACI in ServiceNow CMDB Ownership.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.6.10' running on snow:cmdb_ci, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:cmdb_ci earliest=-90d
| where match(name,"(?i)prod)")
| stats latest(sys_updated_on) as last_update, values(managed_by) as owners by name
| lookup security_raci.csv ci_name AS name OUTPUT expected_owner
| where NOT match(mvjoin(owners,"|"), expected_owner)
description: Correlates CI ownership role changes with expected RACI contacts so responsibilities for assets remain traceable.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for ISO 27001 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes itsm and sourcetypes snow:cmdb_ci. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Maintain RACI lookup from ISO SoA; (2) normalize CMDB fields; (3) quarterly access governance review. Indexes required: `index=itsm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=snow:cmdb_ci`.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `snow:cmdb_ci`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain RACI lookup from ISO SoA; (2) normalize CMDB fields; (3) quarterly access governance review.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:cmdb_ci earliest=-90d
| where match(name,"(?i)prod)")
| stats latest(sys_updated_on) as last_update, values(managed_by) as owners by name
| lookup security_raci.csv ci_name AS name OUTPUT expected_owner
| where NOT match(mvjoin(owners,"|"), expected_owner)
```

Understanding this SPL

**Security Role Changes vs RACI in ServiceNow CMDB Ownership (A.5.2)** — Correlates CI ownership role changes with expected RACI contacts so responsibilities for assets remain traceable.

Documented **Data sources**: `snow:cmdb_ci`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:cmdb_ci. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype=snow:cmdb_ci, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(name,"(?i)prod)")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where NOT match(mvjoin(owners,"|"), expected_owner)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Bar chart
grandmaExplanation: We watch for correlates ci ownership role changes with expected raci contacts so responsibilities for assets remain traceable. This helps maintain steady compliance posture and reduces manual review effort.
